title: CAB approval bypass — change pushed before scheduled window
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate emergency changes with a retro-CR (handled explicitly via approval_type=emergency).
evidence: Saved search 'cab_bypass' (hourly) emits pipeline apply events outside the CR scheduled window.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Direct evidence of an unapproved change path.
compliance.controlObjective: Detects violations of SOC-2 CC8.1 (Change management) — Splunk UC-22.42.4: CAB approval bypass — change pushed before scheduled window.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A deployment pipeline applies a change 2 hours before the CR scheduled window; the UC fires within the hour.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Pipeline apply inside the scheduled window; no alert.

dataSources: CI/CD pipeline apply events tagged with change_id, ServiceNow CR schedule data.
spl:
index=change sourcetype=prod:apply earliest=-1h
| join change_id [search index=itsm sourcetype=servicenow:change | fields change_id window_start window_end approval_type]
| eval start_s=strptime(window_start,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), end_s=strptime(window_end,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| where approval_type!="emergency" AND (_time<start_s OR _time>end_s)
| table _time change_id applied_by window_start window_end
description: Correlates every production apply event to its CR scheduled window and emits those that landed outside the declared window. Out-of-window changes that are not flagged as emergency are a primary ITGC deficiency.
value: Turns CAB approval from a calendar event into an operational control with per-change evidence.
implementation: (1) Force CI/CD pipelines to emit change_id; (2) ingest CR schedule data; (3) run hourly; (4) SOAR auto-freezes the pipeline on hit. Indexes required: `index=change`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=prod:apply`, `sourcetype=servicenow:change`.
visualization: Table of out-of-window applies, bar chart of offending pipelines, single-value '% of changes applied in window'. Key fields: `applied_by`, `change_id`, `start_s`, `window_end`, `window_start`.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools

knownFalsePositives: Baseline drift during scheduled patches, controlled config updates, or planned upgrades.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CI/CD pipeline apply events tagged with change_id, ServiceNow CR schedule data..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Force CI/CD pipelines to emit change_id; (2) ingest CR schedule data; (3) run hourly; (4) SOAR auto-freezes the pipeline on hit.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=change sourcetype=prod:apply earliest=-1h
| join change_id [search index=itsm sourcetype=servicenow:change | fields change_id window_start window_end approval_type]
| eval start_s=strptime(window_start,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), end_s=strptime(window_end,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| where approval_type!="emergency" AND (_time<start_s OR _time>end_s)
| table _time change_id applied_by window_start window_end
```

Understanding this SPL

**CAB approval bypass — change pushed before scheduled window** — Turns CAB approval from a calendar event into an operational control with per-change evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: CI/CD pipeline apply events tagged with change_id, ServiceNow CR schedule data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: change; **sourcetype**: prod:apply. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=change, sourcetype=prod:apply, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **start_s** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where approval_type!="emergency" AND (_time<start_s OR _time>end_s)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: _time, applied_by, change_id, start_s, window_end, window_start.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with change or CMDB source-of-truth and compare with the GRC system so baseline drift matches approved changes.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of out-of-window applies, bar chart of offending pipelines, single-value '% of changes applied in window'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when configs drift from what you baselined, so only approved change moves through.
